item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with The Real John Smith: Addendum 2 was awful. Other than that, this is wonderful. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure about that second Addendum. Still, a +1 because this thing, up to that point, really ranked up there on the creepiness scale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept of this SCP is fascinatingly eerie, but I think that I prefer the interview; Andrew is an interesting character and his story of the wardrobe, his grandfather and himself remind me of a "good o'l campfire-tale".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This feels like a solid Series-1. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing the above comments. This started out promising and delivered. The interview with the unfortunate inheritor was unexpectedly moving. He had had a bond with his grandfather, which was good; the bond was an eldritch abomination, which was not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good. Its item is interesting, the story told by the interview log is compelling, the character is believable. Solid work. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remembered reading this as a draft. It was good then, and it continues to be good. Very nice job. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's really high praise coming from you, Roget. Thanks. This will be the second time I've posted this idea and I'm much happier with it this time around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed the internal logic this had. From the start of the description, the way the damage was described, all through the rest of the article, it feels like not a single word was wasted. It doesn't show its whole hand but just enough to get the mind spinning. Interview log had a lot of personality on display. Containment procedures set up the rest of the article very elegantly. I wonder if the shadow creature would be friends with the one from SCP-122…. +1
